items.title: Hotspot proof depends on visible action, not only device rollout
items.name: Hotspot proof depends on visible action, not only device rollout
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether municipal value proof grows only when deployment, cleanup, and enforcement outcomes are connected into one operating loop rather than measured as separate activities.
items.opportunity: Clarify which combined product changes most improve visible resident outcomes and renewal credibility across councils.

linkedInitiatives.description: Coordinated Cleanup Loop exists to connect enforcement, task dispatch, mobile closure, and recurrence analysis into one municipal operating loop.

assignments.how: Leads evidence synthesis on what creates visible municipal value and renewal proof.
Adds field workflow evidence about task quality and closure reliability.
Adds deployment and relocation evidence needed to understand recurrence reduction.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Municipal leaders and services will expand faster when the product proves cleaner sites and visible action, not only more detections.
evidenceToCollect: Which proof points most influence renewal and expansion decisions.
Whether service and enforcement leaders agree on what counts as visible value.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The investment is worth scaling when it increases expansion and retention without requiring high-touch custom reporting for every municipality.
evidenceToCollect: Which parts of value proof can be standardized.
How much customer-success effort current proof demands.

plannedActivities: Compare mature and immature deployments on recurrence, cleanup speed, and renewal confidence.
Replay the end-to-end flow from detection to service closure on repeat hotspots.
Test which dashboard narratives actually change municipal stakeholder decisions.
items.decisionToMake: Decide which operational metrics and workflow changes should become the default municipal proof pack next quarter.

items.title: Enforceable Case Flow: operating discovery
items.name: Enforceable Case Flow: operating discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether a more guided case workflow can raise case throughput without harming evidentiary rigor or creating legal-review bottlenecks.
items.opportunity: Clarify how much procedural automation municipal teams trust and where legal specialists still need explicit control.

linkedInitiatives.description: Enforceable Case Flow exists to increase the share of qualified incidents that become usable sanctions or partner referrals while reducing officer paperwork.

assignments.how: Leads workflow framing, procedural tradeoff analysis, and recommendation.
Adds evidence integrity, triage, and governance evidence.
Adds treasury and partner handoff evidence needed to close the loop.

riskFocus.question: Can customers figure out how to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Operators can complete more cases with less guidance if the workflow exposes the right next step and document set at each stage.
evidenceToCollect: Where operators still leave the guided flow for side work.
Which steps remain unclear or too rigid.

riskFocus.question: Can we build it within given resources and constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The team can automate more of the case path without locking itself into one-off local procedure variants.
evidenceToCollect: Which local procedural differences are truly reusable.
Where manual escape hatches are still required.

plannedActivities: Compare case drop-off before and after guided workflow adoption.
Map local procedure variants across target municipalities.
Review evidence-access and notice-generation pain points with operators.
items.decisionToMake: Decide which parts of the case flow become mandatory defaults next quarter and which remain configurable exceptions.
